hawkish_phrases: risks to euro area activity had become more balanced, while remaining tilted to the downside
the risk of deflation and of an unanchoring of inflation expectations had largely disappeared
removing the downward bias regarding interest rates would be in line with a gradual and cautious adjustment
negative scenarios, which could trigger further monetary policy action, were assessed to have become less likely
dovish_phrases: a very substantial degree of monetary accommodation continued to be needed
underlying inflation pressures had remained subdued and signs of a convincing upward trend were still lacking
measures of underlying inflation remained low and were expected to rise only gradually over the medium term
one could not be sufficiently confident that inflation would converge towards levels consistent with the Governing Council's aim in a durable and self-sustaining manner
patience and maintaining a steady hand remained warranted at the current meeting
summary: The Governing Council held rates unchanged with continued asset purchases at €60bn monthly from April, maintaining full forward guidance including easing bias, while acknowledging the recovery was firming and deflationary risks had largely disappeared but judging underlying inflation pressures remained too subdued to warrant any policy adjustment.
